title: Greens call for ban on US military using Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: The party will urge MSPs to ‘stand up to Trump’ in a debate this week.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Prestwick is often used by military aircraft for refuelling PA Media The US military should be banned from using Glasgow Prestwick Airport, the Scottish Greens have said ahead of a debate at Holyrood this week.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The party will use its parliamentary time to call on the US military to be barred from publicly-owned airports, saying Holyrood must “stand up to Trump”. Military aircraft regularly use Prestwick, which offers refuelling services and rest facilities for crews.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Greens call for ban on US military using Prestwick Airport
